title: Kubernetes workload baseline: securityContext, probes, and resources
summary: Reference Deployment shape combining non-root execution, health probes, and CPU/memory requests/limits for predictable operations.
body:
Apply this pattern to Deployments and similar workload controllers so deterministic policy checks (non-root, probes, resource limits) align with a single maintainable template. Prefer a shared base patch or Helm values over one-off edits per service.

The snippet below is illustrative: adjust images, names, and probe paths to your app. Keep `runAsNonRoot` and explicit `runAsUser` where your image supports it; set `readOnlyRootFilesystem` when the workload allows.

steps: Merge securityContext, liveness/readiness probes, and resources into the Pod template.
Re-run policy checks in CI after manifest changes.
remediation.example_manifest_snippet:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 65532
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: registry.example/app@sha256:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            capabilities:
              drop: ["ALL"]
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "512Mi"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 8080
remediation.verification: Confirm `policy_check.py` passes for the updated manifest; spot-check `kubectl describe pod` for probe and resource fields.

nist.name: Configuration Settings
nist.discussion: Baseline manifests encode approved configuration for workloads.

nist.name: Boundary Protection
nist.discussion: Least-privilege container settings reduce blast radius at the workload boundary.
